title: quercus × smallii
intro: Quercus × smallii, also known as the Small's white oak, is a hybrid of Quercus bicolor and Quercus alba, resulting in a unique and valuable tree species. The bark and leaves of Quercus × smallii contain various compounds with medicinal properties, including flavonoids, tannins, and saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Quercus × smallii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Quercus × smallii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and combat diseases.
